title: What if your brokerage account was the agent?
author: BuildwithPublic
selftext:
What if you just described what or how you wanted to trade and the agent handled the rest?



At Public we launched Agentic this week - AI agents that live directly inside your brokerage account. You describe your strategy in plain language. The agent monitors the market, manages your cash, and executes your trades. Deterministic. Transparent. Every action visible in your activity feed. You approve every agent before it goes live and can pause or kill it anytime. No code, API keys, or third-party connections.



This isn't a wrapper on top of a broker. This is the broker.



We're going live on X Spaces - 4/9 at 4:30pm ET for an open discussion.



Link in comments.

comments.author: Otherwise_Wave9374
comments.body: Interesting idea, putting the agent inside the brokerage is a way cleaner trust model than "connect your account to a random bot". How are you thinking about constraints and verification, like position sizing limits, max drawdown rules, and making sure the agent cannot drift from the strategy? Also curious what the transparency layer looks like beyond an activity feed, like a rationale + policy trace would be huge. If you are building agentic systems with hard guardrails, https://www.agentixlabs.com/ has some good patterns on bounded agents and approvals.
